entries.description: Selects the lightest safe workflow path, relevant directives/skills, and handoff requirements based on task intent, risk, and touched surfaces.
Preserves architecture DAG boundaries for imports, exports, packages, services, shared code, and dependency direction.
Guides progressive codebase orientation with the SAFE pattern before implementation, review, or unfamiliar work.
Compresses task state at directive or session boundaries so later phases can continue from a compact, current-state handoff instead of drifting through accumulated chat history.
Captures repeated mistakes in durable error memory only when recurrence and prevention criteria are met.
Supports investigation and option discovery before committing to an implementation approach.
Captures durable decisions for repo policy, architecture, workflow, and cross-cutting conventions.
Requires written specifications for features or changes large enough that build-and-see would risk rework.
Frames non-trivial, ambiguous, high-risk, or cross-cutting tasks before substantial edits.
Defines RED/GREEN/REFACTOR expectations for behavior-changing implementation work and bug fixes.
Requires type and contract definition before implementation in typed projects or public API work.
Requires structured evidence of correctness before quality gates and pull requests.
Keeps mutable work in an isolated workspace by detecting existing isolation first, preferring native tools, and falling back to git worktrees only when needed.
Load when changes touch imports, exports, public APIs, file moves, packages, services, layers, shared code, dependency direction, cycles, or the user asks if architecture boundaries still hold.
Load when the user asks to review a PR, branch, diff, local changes, or says approve, merge, or check this change for bugs, regressions, security, maintainability, or merge risk.
Load when reviewing TypeScript/JavaScript refactors, cleanup, AI-generated code, shared utilities, dead code, duplication, complexity, circular dependencies, Fallow output, or maintainability drift.
Load when the user has a PRD, issue, acceptance criteria, or requirements doc and wants a staged implementation task list with relevant files, tests, validation steps, and review checkpoints.
Load when the user wants to turn a feature idea, product request, vague requirement, or problem statement into a concrete PRD/spec before implementation planning or coding.

Load when reviewing changes that may affect production safety: persistence, migrations, external services, async jobs, auth/security/privacy, infra/config/deploy, critical user paths, performance/scale, or cross-service compatibility.
Load when implementation is past GREEN/REFACTOR and the user asks for pre-PR verification, self-audit, scope check, weakest-assumption review, anomaly triage, or a confidence check.
Load when the user asks whether implementation matches a spec, requirements doc, acceptance criteria, or design plan, or says check what is missing, incomplete, or divergent before merge.
Load when the user reports a bug, failing test, CI/build/lint/typecheck failure, regression, flaky behavior, unexpected behavior, or asks to fix a failure or root-cause it.
Load when the user asks to write or review tests, TDD cases, eval scenarios, coverage, assertions, or mocks, or says tests are shallow, flaky, brittle, or too close to implementation.
